title: FATHIYA Tool Selection and Routing
summary: Tool selection is automatic within FATHIYA. The system routes tasks to the appropriate tool based on context, domain, and task classification without manual operator intervention.
key_principles: Tool selection is context-driven, not menu-driven.
Decision tree logic maps task type to tool capabilities.
Routing considers domain, complexity, approval requirements, and adapter availability.
Unknown or ambiguous tasks route to the approval queue for operator decision.
Tool contracts define allowed actions, side effects, and receipt requirements per tool.
boundary: Tool selection metadata only. Does not activate external tools or trigger side effects.
